wikitext:
{{Sequel Disambiguation}}{{Card Infobox|Apotheosis||2|}}{{Keyword Infobox|Innate|2}}{{Keyword Infobox|Upgrade|2}}{{Keyword Infobox|Exhaust|2}}
{{C|Apotheosis||2}} is a 2 {{Icon|CE|2}} cost {{QueryLink|Cards|color:Colorless|Colorless|2}} {{QueryLink|Cards|rarity:Ancient|Ancient|2}} {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Skill|Skill|2}} Card. It {{KW|Upgrade|Upgrades|2}} every card in the player's hand, draw pile, discard pile, and exhaust pile for the remainder of combat, then {{KW|Exhaust|Exhausts|2}} itself. It has {{KW|Innate|Innate|2}}, meaning it will always appear in your first hand of combat. 

== Obtaining Apotheosis==
Can be obtained by taking {{R|Jewelry Box||2}} from {{KW|Nonupeipe||2}}'s offering at the start of [[StS2:Acts#Act 3|Act 3]].

== Interactions ==
=== Order ===
* Cards that are created ''after'' {{C|Apotheosis||2}} has already been played (such as the {{C|Shiv|Shivs|2}} created by {{C|Blade Dance||2}}, the random {{QueryLink|Cards|rarity:Common|Common|2}} cards created by {{C|Hello World||2}}, or cards generated by {{P|Attack Potion||2}}, {{P|Skill Potion||2}}, or {{P|Power Potion||2}}) will not be {{KW|Upgrade|upgraded|2}}.
* Playing {{C|Apotheosis||2}} while having {{R|Burning Sticks||2}} active will upgrade your cards before the copy of {{C|Apotheosis||2}} is created, so the copy will not be upgraded.
* {{C|Apotheosis||2}} does not upgrade itself.

=== Neutral ===
* {{C|Armaments||2}} and {{C|Drain Power||2}} may only be able to {{KW|Upgrade|upgrade|2}} cards before {{C|Apotheosis||2}} is played.
** If the player has cards or other effects that generate cards in the middle of combat, then {{C|Armaments||2}} and {{C|Drain Power||2}} will be able to {{KW|Upgrade|upgrade|2}} them as normal since they will not have been {{KW|Upgrade|upgraded|2}} by {{C|Apotheosis||2}}.

== Trivia ==
* "Apotheosis" is the act of becoming divine or god-like<ref>https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apotheosis</ref>.
* The card art for Apotheosis is reminiscent of the art for the Priest talent of the same name in Blizzard Entertainment's ''World of Warcraft''<ref>https://warcraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Apotheosis</ref> and may have been the inspiration for its art in Slay the Spire (2).
** The same art from World of Warcraft was re-used for the Hearthstone card of the same name<ref>https://hearthstone.wiki.gg/wiki/Apotheosis</ref>, also developed by Blizzard Entertainment and set in the Warcraft universe.

== Update History ==
*'''Early Access v0.98'''
**Added Apotheosis to the game.
